lesson: Uneven ice formation or hollow cubes on one side of the evaporator is caused by a clogged water distribution tube. Swapping the inlet valve or the evaporator plate is an expensive and useless action.
symptoms: Ice cubes are hollow, misshapen, or only forming on one side of the grid.
Water is running continuously into the drain.
The harvest cycle runs but produces very few usable cubes.

tests.temp-probe: Evaporator plate temperature reads 10°F (-12°C) during freeze cycle.
tests.inspect-beater: N/A (no beater). Visual inspection shows water is only trickling down the right side of the evaporator plate; the left side is dry.
tests.continuity-test: Water inlet valve and purge pump solenoids show correct resistance (both ~1.2k ohms).

flavour: Unclogged the water bar. Now it rains cubes.
authenticityNote: Ice machines pump water over a vertical evaporator plate using a distributor tube with small holes. Over time, scale or algae clogs some of these holes, leading to uneven water flow, hollow cubes, and long freeze/harvest times.
